title: Research Assistant
summary: Research Assistant in Wearable Neural Interfaces.
details: If you are interested in wearable neurotechnology and want hands-on experience at the forefront of neural engineering, this position offers a unique opportunity to help develop the experimental and technical foundations of a new research program. You will contribute to adaptive wearable neural-interface research by developing wireless wearable systems for non-invasive electromyography, support the establishment of human-experiment infrastructure, and work at the interface between embedded electronics, biomedical signal acquisition, and neural-interface research.

title: PhD opportunities in neural engineering
summary: Research opportunities in neural interfaces, biomedical signal processing, and translational neurotechnology.
details: Suitable backgrounds include biomedical engineering, electrical engineering, signal processing, robotics, computer science, or related fields.
Projects may involve non-invasive neural interfacing, high-density electrophysiology, closed-loop systems, and computational modeling.
Formal openings may be advertised separately through university channels.

title: Student projects: MSc and undergraduate research
summary: Project-based opportunities for students interested in neural engineering, signal processing, biomedical electronics, and assistive technologies.
details: Student projects may involve experiments, algorithm development, wearable systems, or data analysis.
Both exploratory and thesis-aligned projects may be available depending on timing and fit.

title: Visiting researchers and partnerships
summary: Opportunities for visiting researchers, academic and industry collaborators, and translational partnerships across neural interfaces and neurotechnology.
details: Potential collaboration areas include neural interfaces, motor decoding, rehabilitation technologies, and human-machine interaction.
Please get in touch with a short description of your background and proposed collaboration.

title: Postdoctoral researchers
summary: Postdoctoral openings may be announced as the lab grows and funding opportunities develop.
details: Researchers with strong experience in neurotechnology, biomedical signal processing, machine learning, or translational systems are encouraged to reach out informally.
